title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: But the {{c1::economic impact seems}}(经济影响似乎) to be {{c2::dramatically behind}}(大幅落后). It’s very difficult to {{c3::make sense of}}(理解), how can the model, {{c4::on the one hand}}(一方面), do these amazing things, and then {{c5::on the other hand}}(另一方面), repeat itself twice in some situation?
original: But the economic impact seems to be dramatically behind. It’s very difficult to make sense of, how can the model, on the one hand, do these amazing things, and then on the other hand, repeat itself twice in some situation?
translation: 但经济影响似乎远远落后。这很难理解，模型怎么可能在一方面做出这些令人惊叹的事情，另一方面却在某些情况下重复自己两次？
note:
phrases: economic impact seems | dramatically behind | make sense of | on the one hand | on the other hand
title: Ilya-Sutskever
source: 
chunk: chunk_0004_b5466e
source_lang: en
target_lang: zh
phrase_types: discourse_organizer | reusable_high_frequency_chunk
transfer: Useful for organizing argument flow across longer explanations.
expression_transfer: Useful for organizing argument flow across longer explanations.

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: There’ll be very strong {{c1::economic forces for}}(经济力量) this, and I think the {{c2::impact is going to be felt}}(影响将会被感受到) very strongly. Dwarkesh Patel When {{c3::do you expect that}}(你预计会) impact?
original: There’ll be very strong economic forces for this, and I think the impact is going to be felt very strongly. Dwarkesh Patel When do you expect that impact?
translation: 这背后会有非常强大的经济力量推动，我认为它的影响会非常显著。Dwarkesh Patel 你预计什么时候会出现这种影响？
note:
phrases: economic forces for | impact is going to be felt | do you expect that
title: Ilya-Sutskever

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Dwarkesh Patel {{c1::Should we actually begin}}(我们是否应该开始) here? I {{c2::think this is}}(认为这是) an {{c3::interesting discussion}}(一个有趣的讨论).
original: Dwarkesh Patel Should we actually begin here? I think this is an interesting discussion.
translation: Dwarkesh Patel 我们是否应该从这里开始？我认为这是一个有趣的讨论。

phrases: Should we actually begin | think this is | interesting discussion

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: I have {{c1::two possible explanations}}(有两种可能的解释). The more {{c2::whimsical explanation}}(奇思妙想的解释) is that maybe {{c3::RL training makes}}(强化学习训练使得) the models a little too {{c4::single-minded and narrowly focused}}(目标单一且专注于狭窄领域), {{c5::a little bit too unaware}}(对其他情况有些过于无知), even though it also makes them aware in some other ways.
original: I have two possible explanations. The more whimsical explanation is that maybe RL training makes the models a little too single-minded and narrowly focused, a little bit too unaware, even though it also makes them aware in some other ways.
translation: 我有两个可能的解释。更有趣的解释是，也许强化学习训练让模型变得过于专注和狭隘，在某种程度上让它们变得不那么敏感，尽管它也让它们在其他方面变得更加敏感。
note:
phrases: two possible explanations | whimsical explanation | RL training makes | single-minded and narrowly focused | a little bit too unaware
title: Ilya-Sutskever

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Dwarkesh Patel {{c1::Another thing that’s}}(还有一点是) crazy is how normal {{c2::the slow takeoff}}(缓慢的起步) feels. The idea that we’d be investing 1% of GDP in AI, {{c3::I feel like}}(我认为) it would have felt like {{c4::a bigger deal}}(更大的问题), whereas right now it just feels...
original: Dwarkesh Patel Another thing that’s crazy is how normal the slow takeoff feels. The idea that we’d be investing 1% of GDP in AI, I feel like it would have felt like a bigger deal, whereas right now it just feels...
translation: Dwarkesh Patel 另一个令人难以置信的地方在于，慢速发展的过程看起来竟然如此普通。如果我们将投资 AI 的比例提高到 GDP 的 1%，我感觉这应该会是一件大事，然而现在却只是觉得...
note:
phrases: Another thing that’s | the slow takeoff | I feel like | a bigger deal
title: Ilya-Sutskever

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Ilya Sutskever Sure. Dwarkesh Patel I think your point, about how from the {{c1::average person’s point}}(普通人的观点) of view {{c2::nothing is that different}}(并没有什么不同), will {{c3::continue being true}}(继续保持真实) even into the singularity.
original: Ilya Sutskever Sure. Dwarkesh Patel I think your point, about how from the average person’s point of view nothing is that different, will continue being true even into the singularity.
translation: Ilya Sutskever 当然。Dwarkesh Patel 我认为你的观点，即从普通人的角度来看，现在和未来在奇点到来后都不会有太大的不同，这一点会一直持续下去。
note:
phrases: average person’s point | nothing is that different | continue being true

original: I think the impact of AI is going to be felt. AI is going to be diffused through the economy.
translation: 我认为 AI 的影响会逐渐显现出来。AI 将会在整个经济中得到广泛应用。

text: Ilya Sutskever — {{c1::We're moving from}}(我们正在从) {{c2::the age of}}(那个时代过渡) scaling to the age of research – Explaining model jaggedness Ilya Sutskever {{c3::You know what’s}}(你知道什么) crazy? Dwarkesh Patel Meaning what?

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: and {{c1::brings back the}}(让我想起了) first bug, and you {{c2::can alternate between}}(可以在两者之间切换) those. How {{c3::is that possible}}(这可能吗)? I’m not sure, but it {{c4::does suggest that}}(这确实表明) something strange is going on.
original: and brings back the first bug, and you can alternate between those. How is that possible? I’m not sure, but it does suggest that something strange is going on.

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Then you {{c1::tell the model}}(告诉模型), “Can you {{c2::please fix the}}(请修好这个) bug?” And the model says, “Oh my God, you’re so right. I have a bug. Let me {{c3::go fix that}}(去修好那个).”
original: Then you tell the model, “Can you please fix the bug?” And the model says, “Oh my God, you’re so right. I have a bug. Let me go fix that.”
translation: 然后你告诉模型：“能不能帮我修复这个错误？”模型说：“哦我的天，你完全正确。我确实有个错误。让我去修复它。”
note:
phrases: tell the model | please fix the | go fix that

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: This is {{c1::one of the}}(其中一个) {{c2::very confusing things}}(非常令人困惑的事情) about {{c3::the models right now}}(目前的模型). How to {{c4::reconcile the fact}}(调和这个事实) that they are {{c5::doing so well}}(表现这么好) on evals?
original: This is one of the very confusing things about the models right now. How to reconcile the fact that they are doing so well on evals?
translation: 这正是当前模型中非常令人困惑的一点。如何调和它们在评估中表现如此出色这一事实？
note:
phrases: one of the | very confusing things | the models right now | reconcile the fact | doing so well

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Then you tell it, “You {{c1::have this new}}(拥有这个新) second bug,” and it tells you, “Oh my God, how {{c2::could I have}}(我能) done it? You’re so right again,”
original: Then you tell it, “You have this new second bug,” and it tells you, “Oh my God, how could I have done it? You’re so right again,”
translation: 然后你告诉它：“你又出现了一个新的第二个bug。”它就会说：“天哪，我怎么会犯这种错呢？你又是对的。”

phrases: have this new | could I have

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: How to {{c1::reconcile the fact}}(调和这个事实) that they are {{c2::doing so well}}(表现这么好) on evals? You look at the evals and you go, “Those are {{c3::pretty hard evals}}(相当严格的评估).”
original: How to reconcile the fact that they are doing so well on evals? You look at the evals and you go, “Those are pretty hard evals.”
translation: 如何调和它们在评估中表现如此出色这一事实呢？你来看看这些评估，就会想：“这些评估题目挺难的呀。”

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Ilya Sutskever The thing which I was referring to not feeling different is, okay, such and such company announced some difficult-to-comprehend {{c1::dollar amount of investment}}(投资金额). I don’t think anyone knows {{c2::what to do with}}(如何处理) that.
original: Ilya Sutskever The thing which I was referring to not feeling different is, okay, such and such company announced some difficult-to-comprehend dollar amount of investment. I don’t think anyone knows what to do with that.
translation: 伊利亚·苏茨克弗 我提到的那种感觉不一样的事，就是某某公司宣布了一笔难以理解的巨额投资。我认为没人知道该如何处理这笔投资。
note:
phrases: dollar amount of investment | what to do with

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Back when people were {{c1::doing pre-training}}(进行预训练), {{c2::the question of}}(的问题) what {{c3::data to train}}(训练数据) on was answered, because that answer was everything. When you do pre-training, {{c4::you need all}}(你需要所有) the data.
original: Back when people were doing pre-training, the question of what data to train on was answered, because that answer was everything. When you do pre-training, you need all the data.
translation: 在人们进行预训练时，训练数据的选择问题已经有了答案，因为答案就是所有数据。当你进行预训练时，你需要所有数据。
note:
phrases: doing pre-training | the question of | data to train | you need all

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: From what I hear, all the companies have teams that just produce {{c1::new RL environments}}(新的强化学习环境) and just add it to the {{c2::training mix}}(训练混合). The question is, well, {{c3::what are those}}(那些是什么)?
original: From what I hear, all the companies have teams that just produce new RL environments and just add it to the training mix. The question is, well, what are those?
translation: 据我了解，所有公司都有团队专门开发新的强化学习环境，并将其加入训练混合中。问题是，这些环境到底是什么？
note:
phrases: new RL environments | training mix | what are those

original: But there is another explanation. Back when people were doing pre-training, the question of what data to train on was answered, because that answer was everything.
translation: 但还有另一种解释。在人们进行预训练时，训练数据的选择问题已经有了答案，因为那个答案是所有数据。

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: But when people do {{c1::RL training}}(强化学习训练), they do need to think. They say, “Okay, we want to have this {{c2::kind of}}(有点) RL training for this thing and that kind of RL training for that thing.”
